mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates above 9.7% indicate deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days due to reduced consumer spending expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with UNRATE > 9.7% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns drops below -0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and falling interest rates (FEDFUNDS) create uncertainty about monetary policy effectiveness, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS does not significantly predict SPX returns (p > 0.10) or the combined signal fails to explain >15% of return variance.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) indicate robust demand for durable goods, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in periods with rising HOUST are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals weak supply-side capacity utilization, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days due to reduced corporate profitability.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in periods with falling INDPRO are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's IC falls below -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) reflect strong consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through increased corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with rising RSAFS are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Strong job growth (PAYEMS) indicates a tightening labor market, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days due to inflationary pressures and potential rate hikes.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with rising PAYEMS are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's IC falls below -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong demand-side momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through sustained economic expansion.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between GDPC1 and HOUST does not significantly predict SPX returns (p > 0.10) or the combined signal fails to explain >10% of return variance.
